name: PTC ThingWorx Industrial IoT API
what_it_does: PTC ThingWorx REST and WebSocket APIs for industrial IoT and connected product platform. Enables AI agents to manage IoT device and equipment data ingestion and retrieval for connected product monitoring, handle real-time sensor stream processing and alert generation for industrial operations, access digital twin model data for product lifecycle and asset management, retrieve equipment performance analytics and anomaly detection results, manage edge computing data from ThingWorx Edge SDKs, handle predictive maintenance model output and maintenance recommendation data, access supply chain visibility data from connected products and shipments, retrieve quality inspection and OEE data from manufacturing operations, manage application mashup and dashboard data for industrial visualization, and integrate IIoT data with ERP, PLM, and enterprise analytics platforms.
use_cases: Retrieving connected equipment data from industrial monitoring agents
Processing IoT sensor streams from predictive maintenance agents
Accessing digital twin data from product analytics agents
Monitoring OEE metrics from manufacturing optimization agents
not_for: Consumer IoT without industrial manufacturing and connected product focus
Simple device monitoring without enterprise IIoT platform complexity
Non-manufacturing industries without industrial equipment connectivity needs
best_when: A manufacturer or industrial company using ThingWorx wants AI agents to automate equipment monitoring, predictive maintenance, connected product analytics, quality tracking, and IIoT data integration.
avoid_when: SAFETY RISK: ThingWorx connects to industrial equipment with physical safety implications — automated control actions via ThingWorx without safety interlock validation can cause equipment damage or safety incidents. Automated edge-to-cloud data routing must maintain data integrity for safety-critical monitoring applications. Industrial equipment control must follow Management of Change (MOC) and OSHA PSM procedures.

auth.notes: ThingWorx uses Application Key (API key) and OAuth 2.0. Developer portal at developer.ptc.com. Edge SDKs for device connectivity (Java, C, JavaScript). Webhooks via ThingWorx subscriptions. REST API with JSON and XML. Cloud-hosted and on-premise deployment. Boston, Massachusetts HQ (PTC Inc.). PTC acquired by Rockwell Automation partnership (minority stake).

pricing.free_tier_limits: ThingWorx developer sandbox available; production requires license
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual license per connected thing and user; enterprise pricing for manufacturing deployments
pricing.notes: Boston, Massachusetts. PTC founded 1985. Public (PTC NASDAQ). $2.1B+ annual revenue. Industrial IoT and PLM market. ThingWorx acquired by PTC (2013, $112M). 3,000+ ThingWorx customers. Strong in automotive, aerospace, and industrial manufacturing. Partnership with Rockwell Automation for Connected Enterprise. Competes with Siemens MindSphere and GE Predix for IIoT platform.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per Application Key; documented in developer portal

known_agent_gotchas: SAFETY RISK: ThingWorx equipment control services require safety interlock validation; automated control without MOC procedure and safety review causes industrial safety incidents
Application Key vs. OAuth — ThingWorx primary auth is Application Key (api-key header); OAuth available but Application Key is standard; manage Application Key rotation carefully
Thing vs. Thing Template — ThingWorx data model has Things, Thing Templates, and Thing Shapes; understand hierarchy before querying; wrong level of abstraction returns empty data
Subscription vs. REST polling — ThingWorx subscriptions (webhooks) are more efficient than polling for data-driven events; implement subscriptions for real-time monitoring
Edge SDK connectivity — ThingWorx Edge SDKs connect edge devices; cloud API provides access to data after edge-to-cloud transmission; understand edge latency for real-time requirements
Composer dependency — ThingWorx application configuration (Things, Properties, Services) is done in ThingWorx Composer; API access depends on correct Composer configuration
agent_readiness.security_notes: Industrial IoT platform. SOC2, IEC62443, GDPR. API key/OAuth2. US/EU. IoT device, equipment, and industrial data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Industrial IoT. SOC2, IEC62443, GDPR. API key. US/EU. Equipment and sensor data.
